ucs.title: [LLM] TA505 / Cl0p MOVEit Transfer exploitation — LEMURLOOT (human2.aspx) webshell deployment
ucs.description: Hunts the signature TA505/Cl0p (a.k.a. GOLD TAHOE, DEV-0950 partner cluster) tradecraft from CVE-2023-34362: SQLi against MOVEit Transfer drops the LEMURLOOT .NET webshell as 'human2.aspx' under the MOVEit wwwroot, then the IIS worker process (w3wp.exe) spawns recon/exfil children and stages stolen file inventory before Cl0p extortion.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_name IN ("human2.aspx","human2.aspx.lnk","_human2.aspx") OR (Filesystem.file_path="*\\MOVEitTransfer\\wwwroot\\*" AND Filesystem.file_name="*.aspx" AND NOT Filesystem.file_name IN ("guestaccess.aspx","login.aspx","human.aspx","machine2.aspx"))) by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.user Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | eval src_signal="webshell_drop" | append [ | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="w3wp.exe" AND (Processes.parent_process="*MOVEitTransfer*" OR Processes.parent_process="*MOVEitDMZ*") AND Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe","wmic.exe","sqlcmd.exe","7z.exe","rclone.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process Processes.process_name Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | eval src_signal="moveit_w3wp_child" ] | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
let moveitWebshell = DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileRenamed","FileModified")
| where FileName in~ ("human2.aspx","human2.aspx.lnk","_human2.aspx")
   or (FolderPath has @"\MOVEitTransfer\wwwroot" and FileName endswith ".aspx" and FileName !in~ ("guestaccess.aspx","login.aspx","human.aspx","machine2.aspx"))
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, ActionType, FolderPath, FileName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessAccountName, SHA256, Signal="LEMURLOOT_drop";
let moveitChildren = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
| where InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any ("MOVEitTransfer","MOVEitDMZ")
| where FileName in~ ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe","wmic.exe","sqlcmd.exe","7z.exe","rclone.exe","tasklist.exe","ipconfig.exe")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, Signal="MOVEit_w3wp_child";
union moveitWebshell, moveitChildren
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: human2.aspx in the MOVEit wwwroot is a unique LEMURLOOT artifact tied to TA505/Cl0p mass-extortion campaigns (May–June 2023, plus reuse). Combining the file-create signal with the IIS app-pool spawning living-off-the-land binaries makes this near-zero-FP and specifically catches Cl0p's SQLi → webshell → recon → rclone-to-MEGA chain rather than generic webshell activity.

ucs.title: [LLM] TA505 Get2/SDBbot delivery — msiexec installing remote MSI from Office or HTML-smuggling chain
ucs.description: Hunts TA505's long-running malspam pattern (Hive0065 / GOLD DRAKE) where Excel/HTML-smuggled lures drop an .iso/.img containing an LNK that runs msiexec /i against an attacker-hosted MSI, fetching the Get2 downloader and SDBbot RAT — a delivery primitive TA505 has reused across FlawedAmmyy, ServHelper, and Cl0p staging.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name="msiexec.exe" AND (Processes.process="*http://*" OR Processes.process="*https://*" OR Processes.process="*\\\\*") AND (Processes.process="* /i *" OR Processes.process="* /package *" OR Processes.process="* -i *") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rex field=process "(?i)(?<msi_url>https?:\/\/[^\s\"']+|\\\\\\\\[^\s\"']+)" | where isnotnull(msi_url) | eval susp_tld=if(match(msi_url,"(?i)\.(top|xyz|click|site|live|info|ru|su|cn|biz|icu|monster|cam|shop|fun)\/") OR match(msi_url,"(?i)https?:\/\/\d+\.\d+\.\d+\.\d+"),1,0), susp_parent=if(match(parent_process_name,"(?i)(outlook|winword|excel|powerpnt|wscript|cscript|mshta|rundll32|explorer|7zfm|isoburn)\.exe"),1,0) | where susp_tld=1 OR susp_parent=1 | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where FileName =~ "msiexec.exe"
| where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s[/-](i|package)\s"
| where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)(https?://|\\\\)"
| extend MsiUrl = extract(@"(?i)(https?://[^\s\"']+|\\\\\\[^\s\"']+)", 1, ProcessCommandLine)
| extend SuspiciousTld = MsiUrl matches regex @"(?i)\.(top|xyz|click|site|live|info|ru|su|cn|biz|icu|monster|cam|shop|fun)/"
| extend BareIp = MsiUrl matches regex @"(?i)https?://\d+\.\d+\.\d+\.\d+"
| extend SuspectParent = InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("outlook.exe","winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","explorer.exe","7zfm.exe","isoburn.exe")
| extend ContainerLure = InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any (".iso",".img",".vhd",".lnk",".html",".htm")
| where SuspiciousTld or BareIp or SuspectParent or ContainerLure
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, ProcessCommandLine, MsiUrl, SuspiciousTld, BareIp, SuspectParent, ContainerLure
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: Microsoft (Hive0065) and Proofpoint reporting consistently tie TA505 to msiexec-based remote MSI installs originating from Office or container-mounted LNKs as the Get2 / SDBbot stager. Pivoting on msiexec with a remote URL plus an Office/script/container parent (instead of a vanilla msiexec rule) targets TA505's specific delivery chain and filters out legitimate enterprise software installs that c
